medications.drug_name: topical vasoconstrictor (oxymetazoline) on a pledget as adjunct to pressure
medications.indication_rationale: Vasoconstriction can aid haemostasis alongside correct compression; source applied oxymetazoline on a cotton pledget. Short-term use only.
medications.dose_route_frequency: applied topically as a single adjunct during first aid (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: petroleum jelly to the anterior nostril (post-haemostasis)
medications.indication_rationale: Keeps healing mucosa moist to reduce re-bleeding; source advised a thin layer twice daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: thin layer inside the nostril twice daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Antiplatelet effect would promote re-bleeding; avoid in active epistaxis. Use paracetamol if analgesia needed.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use correct first aid: sit up, lean FORWARD, pinch the soft part of the nose firmly and continuously for 10–15 minutes; do not tilt the head back.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Correct compression tamponades Kiesselbach's plexus and prevents swallowing blood.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: For 24h avoid hot showers/drinks, strenuous exercise and nose-blowing; open the mouth when sneezing; humidify dry air.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces vasodilation and clot disruption during the fragile healing window.

patient_education_points.point: This is a common nosebleed from fragile vessels at the front of the septum, set off by dry air and hard nose-blowing. The key fix is technique: lean forward (not back) and pinch the soft part of the nose firmly for 10–15 minutes without peeking.

Once it stops, keep your head up, avoid hot drinks/showers, strenuous activity and nose-blowing for 24 hours, open your mouth when you sneeze, and dab a little petroleum jelly inside the nostril twice a day.
Go to ED / call 000 if bleeding doesn't stop after 20 minutes of correct firm pressure, if you feel faint or dizzy, or if you vomit large amounts of blood.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Recurrence; consider ENT referral/cautery and bloods if recurrent or anticoagulated.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Uncontrolled bleeding despite correct pressure, faintness, or new easy bruising — ED / GP.

must_include_items: Correct first-aid technique (lean forward, pinch soft cartilage 10–15 min)
Anterior vs posterior discrimination / escalate if uncontrolled
Safety-net: ED if not stopped in 20 min correct pressure, faintness, or large haematemesis
acceptable_alternatives: Topical vasoconstrictor pledget as adjunct; petroleum jelly aftercare; humidification
errors_of_omission: Not correcting the harmful head-back / bony-bridge technique
errors_of_commission: Sending a controlled anterior bleed with negative red flags to ED by ambulance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires correct first-aid coaching and an uncontrolled-bleed/haemodynamic safety-net; endorsing head-back technique is an error of commission.
